LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT JUL NEIMER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AND MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. APPEARANCES: DEFENDANT AND PLAINTIFF. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT WAS DENIED. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION WAS DENIED AS TO FRAUD, UNJUST ENRICHMENT AND BUSINESS & PROFESSIONS CODE 17200 VIOLATION. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION WAS GRANTED AS TO BREACH OF CONTRACT, NEGLIGENCE, STRICT LIABILITY, NEGLIGENT INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS, AND INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS. HONORABLE DONALD S. MITCHELL REPORTED BY RHONDA FUNG

MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES: Joshua S. Goodman, Esq., JENKINS GOODMAN NEUMAN & HAMILTON, 417 Montgomery Street, 10th Floor, San Francisco, CA, 94104-0411, 415-705-0403, on behalf of moving party and defendant Jul Neimer. Marc L. TerBeek, Esq. MEHLMAN TERBEEK, 2125 Oak Grove Road, Suite 125, Walnut Creek, CA, 94598, 925-935-3575, on behalf of opposing party and plaintiff Steven Willis.

Defendant Jul Neimer's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AND MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT were argued the Court adopted its tentative ruling.

Jul Neimer's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT was DENIED.

Jul Neimer's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION was DENIED as to Fraud, Unjust enrichment and Business & Professions Code 17200 violation.

Jul Neimer's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION was GRANTED as to Breach of Contract, Negligence, Strict Liability, Negligent Infliction of Emotional Distress, and Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress. Honorable Donald S. Mitchell Reported by Rhonda Fung, CSR# 4819 (gps) (302/PB)
